source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from human-powered glider CFRP wing construction practice: CFRP main spar tube with tapered sections, rear subspar at trailing edge, alternating compression/tension ribs, root spar-fuselage fitting, wingtip cap, film-covered skin panels, and wire-bracing ferrules. Load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds inherit verbatim from the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design the wing spar and primary wing structure of a human-powered glider (Human-powered Prop-plane division assumed) for a human-powered aircraft launch benchmark. The total wingspan must not exceed 36 m; this design targets a 25 m wingspan as representative. The aircraft launches from a 10 m high horizontal platform above the lake surface. The pilot is seated in a semi-reclined cockpit. The wing spar must carry a symmetric positive-g flight load of 2.5 g at an assumed gross weight of 120 kg (pilot 70 kg + aircraft 50 kg), giving a design limit load of 300 kg equivalent distributed span-wise in an elliptical lift distribution. The spar must also survive a negative-g load factor of -1.0 g for gusts. A mandatory pre-competition bend test requires the completed wing to support a root bending moment equivalent to the 2.5 g flight load held statically for at least 10 seconds with no audible cracking and no permanent deformation beyond 5 mm at the wingtip on unload. Material is typically a CFRP tube spar with balsa/foam ribs and heat-shrink film skin; the spar CFRP is modeled as linear elastic orthotropic with longitudinal E approximately 130 GPa (pultruded T300/epoxy). Primary verification is linear static structural FEA of the wing spar under the 2.5 g positive and 1.0 g negative load cases, with stress gated by CFRP laminate allowables. A linear buckling eigenvalue analysis is required on the upper skin / spar cap under the positive-g case with minimum load factor 1.5. Vibration (flutter) analysis is relevant in a certification context but is NOT required at this design gate. Thermal, fluid (sectional aerodynamic lift is already reduced to a distributed load on the spar), and radiation analyses are explicitly NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the wing structure, a mass budget, and a report listing total aircraft structural mass in kg, wingtip deflection at 2.5 g in mm, peak spar cap strain, and first buckling load factor.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
This wing is delivered as a multi-part assembly modelled on established human-powered aircraft practice. The speculative part decomposition is: (A) CFRP main spar tube in two telescoping sections joined at midspan, (B) trailing-edge subspar of reduced diameter, (C) compression ribs at high-load stations, (D) tension ribs between compression ribs, (E) steel root spar-fuselage fitting, (F) wingtip cap with integrated nav-light mount, (G) heat-shrink film skin panels bonded to rib perimeters, and (H) wire-bracing ferrules if the design uses jury struts. Inter-family interfaces: spar-to-rib adhesively bonded with fillet, spar-to-root-fitting pinned with shear pin, subspar-to-rib bonded, wingtip-cap-to-spar bonded, skin-to-rib film bonded. The final FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED model only — per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) at the root-fitting pin and at spar-rib bond lines must be ≤ 1.0 under the governing 2.5 g load case. All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria are added.
short_prompt:
Design the wing spar and primary wing structure of a human-powered glider (Prop-plane division) for a human-powered-aircraft launch benchmark, delivered as a multi-part assembly: CFRP main spar tube in two telescoping sections joined at midspan + reduced-diameter trailing-edge subspar + compression ribs at high-load stations + tension ribs between them + steel root spar-fuselage fitting + wingtip cap + heat-shrink film skin panels bonded to rib perimeters + optional wire-bracing ferrules. Wingspan <= 36 m (this design targets 25 m); launch from a 10 m high horizontal platform above a lake; pilot semi-reclined. Spar CFRP modeled linear-elastic orthotropic, longitudinal E ~ 130 GPa (pultruded T300/epoxy).

Gross weight 120 kg (pilot 70 kg + aircraft 50 kg).

Load cases.
- Positive flight load: 2.5 g symmetric (300 kg equivalent distributed elliptically span-wise).
- Negative gust: -1.0 g.
- Pre-competition bend test: static root bending moment at the 2.5 g flight load for >= 10 s with no audible cracking and no permanent wingtip deformation > 5 mm on unload.

A linear buckling eigenvalue on the upper skin / spar cap under positive-g must be >= 1.5. Inter-part DCR at root-fitting pin and at spar-rib bond lines <= 1.0 under 2.5 g.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Elliptical lift distribution comes from lifting-line or panel-method aero, not FEA.

prompt.title: Human-powered glider 25 m wing spar (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Design a 25 m CFRP wing structure as an assembly of main spar tube, trailing-edge subspar, compression and tension ribs, root fitting, wingtip cap, film skin panels, and bracing ferrules; carry 2.5 g flight load with elliptical lift, survive -1 g gust, pass static bend test. FEM verification at assembled level only; connection DCR ≤ 1.0 under governing load case.

load_cases.name: flight_2p5g_positive
load_cases.distribution: elliptical span-wise lift, 2.5 g at 120 kg gross

load_cases.name: static_bend_test
load_cases.description: root bending moment equivalent to LC1, held 10 s

parts.name: CFRP main spar tube
parts.role: primary bending member carrying elliptical lift from root to tip in two telescoping sections

geometric_constraints.taper: linear taper od reduces toward wingtip

parts.material_hint: pultruded T300/epoxy CFRP, longitudinal E approx 130 GPa, tensile allowable 1800 MPa

parts.name: trailing-edge subspar
parts.role: secondary torsion-resisting spar at rear of chord, reacts pitching moment and carries trailing-edge loads
parts.material_hint: CFRP pultruded tube, lower stiffness than main spar

parts.name: compression rib
parts.role: high-load chordwise rib at aileron hinge, jury-strut, and root stations, transfers lift to spar and preserves airfoil
parts.material_hint: balsa sandwich with CFRP cap strips or plywood web

parts.name: tension rib
parts.role: lightweight chordwise rib between compression ribs maintaining airfoil shape under film tension
parts.material_hint: balsa or EPS foam with film skin adhesion surface

parts.name: root spar-fuselage fitting
parts.role: metal fitting transferring root bending moment and shear into fuselage via shear pin

parts.name: wingtip cap
parts.role: closes spar tube end, integrates nav-light mount, protects tip in ground handling
parts.material_hint: balsa/foam core with CFRP veil, bonded to spar

parts.name: heat-shrink film skin panel
parts.role: aerodynamic cover bonded to rib perimeters, transfers distributed lift pressure into ribs
parts.material_hint: polyester heat-shrink covering film (e.g. Oracover/Solartex), bonded with contact adhesive

parts.name: bracing-wire ferrule
parts.role: terminates kevlar/steel bracing wire at rib or spar pick-ups if jury-strut bracing is used
parts.material_hint: swaged aluminium ferrule on 7x7 steel cable or Kevlar rope

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: origin at wing root leading edge, X along chord, Y along span, Z up

pass_fail_criteria.description: Root-fitting shear-pin and spar-rib bondline demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across all inter-part interfaces ≤ 1.0 under 2.5 g positive-g load case.
Assembled wing first-bending mode frequency must exceed the pilot-cadence input band (0-3 Hz) with margin, i.e. first bending mode ≥ 5 Hz for stable flight.

interfaces.load_path: Main spar tube transfers shear and bending into compression ribs via adhesive fillet at spar cutout
Main spar tube transfers root bending moment and shear into root fitting via shear pin in lug
Main spar tube terminates in wingtip cap with adhesive bond
Trailing-edge subspar bonded to compression ribs reacting torsion and trailing-edge loads
Compression ribs and tension ribs share continuous leading-edge and trailing-edge members bonded between them
Tension ribs receive film skin tension from heat-shrink skin panel via perimeter adhesive
Root fitting terminates bracing-wire ferrule via threaded clevis if jury-strut bracing is used

fem_check_policy.rationale: Wing behavior is dominated by spar-rib-skin interaction and root-fitting load transfer; per-part stress is reported for traceability but does not gate pass/fail. The assembled model captures spar buckling, rib-spar bondline stress, and root-pin shear simultaneously.

notes.why_multi_part: A human-powered glider wing cannot be modelled as a single solid body: its CFRP spar, ribs, film skin, and root fitting each carry distinct load paths and are manufactured separately. Typical competition wings use 20-50 discrete components.
notes.speculation_caveats: parts[] counts, interface types, and dimensional ranges are inferred from published HPA design practice (Daedalus, Musashino Birdman Team, Nihon University) and are not taken from a specific rulebook.
